title: Linear Models, Regression, and Systems · Model Lens: Linear Regression
summary: Allocate staff and supplies under multiple linear constraints. Planned artifact: Add and verify the math int linear regression increment in a linear allocation and scenario model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Fit a linear regression and inspect residuals before using its predictions.
Fit and interpret linear rate and intercept parameters with units and domain limits.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-intermediate cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

steps.title: Calculate Linear Regression evidence
steps.instruction: A model passes through (1, 5) and (5, 17). Calculate its average rate of change. Use the result to decide Linear Regression for a linear allocation and scenario model.
steps.why: The Linear Regression result for a linear allocation and scenario model is incomplete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: Use Δoutput/Δinput = (17 − 5) / (5 − 1). Reconcile the result with this competency evidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Fit a linear regression and inspect residuals before using its predictions.
